name: Sierra de Cárdena and Lubián Loop
name_original: La Sierra de Cárdena desde Lubián — circular por Los Lagunas de Garandones
description: This difficult 50.3-mile mountain biking route from Lubián explores the Sierra de Cárdena and Los Lagunas de Garandones, gaining 6561 feet.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Lubián, located in the Alta Sanabria region, offers routes through diverse landscapes bordering natural parks. The area is characterized by river valleys, extensive forests, and hills, providing varied terrain for mountain bike enthusiasts. Proximity to the Sanabria Lake Natural Park and Montesinho Natural Park means routes often feature natural vantage points and scenic paths. This region provides a setting for exploring natural beauty on two wheels. Best mountain bike trails around Lubián The most popular mountain bike trail is Senderos de Los Lagos — circular por la Sierra de Rábano — el Parque Natural del Lago de Sanabria, a 24.7 miles (39.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 5 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Sierra de Rábano within the Sanabria Lake Natural Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de Cárdena and Lubián Loop, a difficult 50.3 miles (80.9 km) path. This route offers extensive views across the Sierra de Cárdena and the surrounding region. Local mountain bikers also love the Sanctuary of La Tuiza – Lubián Village loop from Lubián, a 51.3 miles (82.5 km) trail leading through the local countryside and past cultural landmarks, often completed in about 7 hours 40 minutes. Mountain biking around Lubián is defined by river valleys, dense woodlands, and the rolling hills of the Alta Sanabria region. The network offers options for experienced riders seeking challenging terrain. The routes in Lubián are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 1.0 stars from more than 2 reviews. More than 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Lubián's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Lubián?
A: There are 5 mountain bike trails listed around Lubián on komoot, offering a range of challenging routes through the Alta Sanabria region. These trails explore diverse landscapes, including river valleys, forests, and hills.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails in Lubián?
A: Yes, while most routes are difficult, there is one moderate trail: the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tera River – Lake Sanabria loop from Ribadelago Viejo&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 29.8 km long and typically takes about 2 hours 30 minutes to complete.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Lubián?
A: The terrain around Lubián is characterized by river valleys, dense woodlands, and the rolling hills of the Alta Sanabria region. Routes often feature natural vantage points and scenic paths, providing varied and challenging experiences for mountain bikers.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or viewpoints along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty. You can find stunning features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, especially impressive in spring. Many routes also offer extensive views across areas like the Sierra de Cárdena and within the Sanabria Lake Natural Park, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222816502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senderos de Los Lagos — circular por la Sierra de Rábano&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What cultural landmarks can I see while mountain biking near Lubián?
A: The area offers cultural points of interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of La Tuiza – Lubián Village loop from Lubián&lt;/a&gt; passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of La Tuiza&lt;/a&gt;, a church with a blend of Galician and Zamora architectural styles. The region is also part of the historic Mozarabic Way.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike loops available?
A: Yes, Lubián offers several extensive loops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222915133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Cárdena and Lubián Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 80.9 km path, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of La Tuiza – Lubián Village loop from Lubián&lt;/a&gt; covers 82.5 km.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Lubián?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking in the Alta Sanabria region, with milder temperatures and beautiful natural scenery. Spring is particularly good for seeing waterfalls like Sotillo at their fullest.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Lubián?
A: More than 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Lubián's varied terrain. While the average rating is 1.0 stars from 2 reviews, the area's diverse landscapes, including river valleys and extensive forests, provide a setting for exploring natural beauty on two wheels.

Q: Are there any trails that go through natural parks?
A: Yes, some routes venture into or border natural parks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222816502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senderos de Los Lagos — circular por la Sierra de Rábano&lt;/a&gt; explores the Sierra de Rábano within the Sanabria Lake Natural Park. The region is also close to the vast Montesinho Natural Park.

Q: Can I find lakes or reservoirs along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area around Lubián features several bodies of water. You can find highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Serrada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Payón&lt;/a&gt;, and the prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5451747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;, which are often part of or accessible from mountain bike routes.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Lubián suitable for beginners?
A: Most of the mountain bike trails listed around Lubián are rated as 'difficult' due to their length and elevation changes. There is one 'moderate' route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tera River – Lake Sanabria loop from Ribadelago Viejo&lt;/a&gt;, which might be more suitable for riders with some experience, but true beginner-friendly trails are limited.
tips:
Beautiful panoramic view of the Puente de Porto reservoir that we find at this point on the road, very close to the Marmolín refuge.

If you are doing the La Reina de la Alta Sanabria route, keep in mind that it may or may not be completely cyclable depending on the weather. Water can create swampy areas, making steep climbs difficult or even obliterating the road on untraveled sections. It is a route for experts, with technical parts and sections with loose stones and/or too much vegetation.
An epic mountain of trails around the Cárdena mountain lakes. Be sure to check the weather before heading to the lake region.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult, covering approximately 81 km with nearly 2000 meters of elevation gain. It's best suited for experienced mountain bikers with good physical conditioning and technical skills.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific detailed information about the mountain biking terrain for this exact route is not widely documented, given its 'difficult' rating and location in a mountainous region, you should anticipate varied surfaces. This likely includes a mix of forest tracks, gravel roads, and potentially some technical singletrack with rocky or uneven sections. Be prepared for significant climbs and descents.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or natural features along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features some beautiful natural highlights. You'll pass by the View of Puente de Porto Reservoir, offering scenic vistas, and the tranquil Cárdena Lagoon.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the [CIMA ZM06] Parque Eólico Padornelo * Lubián, Camino Sanabrés (East), Mtb Camino Sanabrés (Este) / Mtb Camiño Xabrés (Leste), and Montaña de Sanabria - Etapa 5, among others.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, given its classification as 'difficult' with a substantial distance and elevation gain, this route is not recommended for beginners. It requires a high level of fitness and mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this route is generally during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the weather is more stable. Summer can be very hot, and winter may bring snow and ice, especially at higher elevations, making conditions challenging and potentially unsafe for mountain biking.

faqs.question: Is La Sierra de Cárdena desde Lubián — circular por Los Lagunas de Garandones dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact mountain biking route are not readily available, it is generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash in natural park areas and on trails to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles and clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the Parque Natural Lago de Sanabria y Sierras Segundera y de Porto. Generally, there are no entrance fees for accessing natural parks in Spain. However, specific activities or certain areas within the park might require permits or have regulations. It's always best to check with the park's official visitor center or website for the most current information regarding any necessary permits or specific rules for mountain biking.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Lubián?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact mountain biking route in Lubián are not detailed, you would typically look for public parking within or on the outskirts of the village. As it's a circular route, starting and ending in Lubián, finding a suitable spot near the village center or a designated parking area would be the most logical approach. Arriving early, especially on weekends, is always a good strategy.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail in Lubián?
faqs.answer: Lubián is a small village, and while specific details for facilities directly on the trail are scarce, you can expect to find basic amenities such as small shops, cafes, or guesthouses within the village itself. It's recommended to check local listings or make reservations in advance, especially if you plan to stay overnight or need specific services.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this difficult route?
faqs.answer: For a difficult, long mountain bike route like this, you should bring essential gear including a well-maintained mountain bike, helmet, appropriate cycling clothing, plenty of water (more than you think you'll need), energy snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, navigation device (like komoot with the downloaded tour), and sun protection. Given the remote nature, consider a power bank for your phone and emergency supplies.
